lede: A look at April 16 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Charlie Chaplin and Pope Benedict XVI, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: South Korea
events.desc: The South Korean ferry MV Sewol capsizes and sinks near Jindo Island, killing 304 passengers and crew and leading to widespread criticism of the South Korean government, media, and shipping authorities.

events.title: Virginia Tech shooting
events.desc: Virginia Tech shooting: Seung-Hui Cho murders 32 people and injures 17 before committing suicide.

events.title: Bangladesh
events.desc: India and Bangladesh begin a five-day border conflict, but are unable to resolve the disputes about their border.

events.title: OECD
events.desc: The Organization of European Economic Co-operation is formed.

events.title: Bernard Baruch
events.desc: Bernard Baruch first applies the term "Cold War" to describe the relationship between the United States and the Soviet Union.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Italian-German Tarigo convoy is attacked and destroyed by British ships.

events.title: Harriet Quimby
events.desc: Harriet Quimby becomes the first woman to fly an airplane across the English Channel.

events.title: French Revolutionary Wars
events.desc: French Revolutionary Wars: The Battle of Mount Tabor: Napoleon drives Ottoman Turks across the River Jordan near Acre.

events.title: Revolt of the Comuneros
events.desc: The Revolt of the Comuneros begins in Spain against the rule of Charles V.

events.title: AD 69
events.desc: Defeated by Vitellius' troops at Bedriacum, Roman emperor Otho commits suicide.

births.name: Charlie Chaplin
births.line: English actor, director, producer, screenwriter, and composer

births.name: Pope Benedict XVI
births.line: Head of the Catholic Church from 2005 to 2013

births.name: John Franklin
births.line: English admiral and politician, fourth Lieutenant Governor of Van Diemen's Land

births.name: Leo Tindemans
births.line: Belgian politician, 43rd Prime Minister of Belgium

births.name: Henri, Grand Duke of Luxembourg
births.line: Grand Duke of Luxembourg from 2000 to 2025

births.name: Selena
births.line: American singer-songwriter, actress, and fashion designer

deaths.text: David Lean, English director, producer, and screenwriter
Yasunari Kawabata, Japanese novelist and short story writer, Nobel Prize laureate
Adelaide del Vasto, regent of Sicily, mother of Roger II of Sicily, queen of Baldwin I of Jerusalem

observances.text: Christian feast day: Isabella Gilmore (Church of England)
Christian feast day: Molly Brant (Konwatsijayenni) (Anglican Church of Canada, Episcopal Church)
